mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines in late-cycle phases yield positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances or predictive accuracy falls below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of occurrences or loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic growth and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals a recessionary shock that immediately erodes equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDP contractions trigger positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases or the signal predictive power vanishes after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment during post-shock recovery phases indicates supply-side normalization, driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances during recovery periods or fails out-of-sample validation.
